analysis.name: Ashutosh Sabharwal
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Ashu is really a nice person and a great researcher. As an advisor, he was a great force in helping me choose good research problems and improving as a scientist.
Amazing, down-to-earth, approachable, and brilliant in research! I recommend him as an advisor.
Ashu is a good mentor and is a top-rated researcher. He brings practical problems to his research and also an industry perspective. I totally recommend him as a PhD guide.

analysis.name: Ron Goldman
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Seriously an evil man.  He never gave any instructions or lectures because he was admittedly lazy.  He got jealous of the popular TA halfway through the semester and would restrict him only to work with a certain unannounced area of the Symonds for each lecture.  Curve works against you - I had a 95 (110 on the final with a solution he found fascinating that he had never though of before) and got an A- in the course.   Tends to be slightly misogynistic and that's not a statement I would make lig
Lectures were unclear.  Professor was unreceptive to questions.  Workload was tremendous (and apparently it's been scaled back from previous years).  Get used to living in Symonds Lab.

He is lazy and doesn't teach AT ALL. There is no teaching in this class whatsoever, so if you if you don't pick up stuff quickly on your own, do not take this class. The TAs are somewhat helpful but you have to wait a long time to see one during class. The exams are hard and the prof will do nothing for you if you are falling behind.

Dr. Goldman told us all semester that we wouldn't have a final exam, and the second-to-the last day of class he announced that there would be one, and it would be closed-note and individual rather than with partners like the class had been. He was pretty unapproachable, and as another reviewer pointed out, mysogynistic.

The only professor I've ever had who I believe to be genuinely evil. As in, wishes ill toward his fellow human beings. The class went from 80-something to 17 or 18 by the end! But i must admit, I learned from him. A good class to have taken, I suppose, but a wretched experience to take.

analysis.name: Andrew Barron
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: exams are tough but you learn a lot -dunno if he is still teaching (I took Inorganic with him)
Hes a real jerk if you have to talk to him. Not nice to interact with at all, quite an unpleasant person.

analysis.name: Andre Droxler
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This class was mainly just him reading off of PowerPoint and us asking questions. Once he got off topic it was hard to get him back on. Enjoyed having him as a professor anc I love his accent. I look forward to going to Belize because of the research he has done there. He is one of the main reasons I'm going to minor in Environmental Studies.
I adore Andre. He's always bubbling with enthusiasm and is thrilled to see you. He's got a pretty thick accent sometimes, but you get used to it, and he has so much to say.
Dr. Droxler is patient and kind and his French accent only enhances his vast knowledge of my earth science class! Talking rocks rocks!!!
Andre is an extremely patient teacher who is full of enthusiasm. Loved taking his courses on Climate change and carbonate geology

analysis.name: Maher Awad
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Maher is the worst teacher I have ever had. He has made several of my peers cry. Doing well in his class requires following his rules, not necessarily mastering the language. Not only does he play favorites, but he also demonizes the students he doesn't like to the point that his class becomes unbearable. He should not be in a position of power.

Maher does not take a laid-back approach to learning Arabic, although he is very funny. He expects you to do the work and he will put you on the spot in class to make sure you're learning. But if you put time into this class, you will learn so much about Arabic, and Maher will be supportive, inspirational, and humorous along the way.

I don't know about all of these previous negative comments about Maher. He is one of the most caring and helpful professors I have had and his class is always very enjoyable. He focuses on getting us to talk more than anything. But when learning a new language, that is more important than anything else.

Definately plays favorites (wear hijab, be fluent in arabic and he will LOVE you). Relies heavily on textbook and DVD. He seems to be bothered whenever someone asks for clarification, stammers, and dances around the question, never answering it. He expects you to be somewhat fluent in arabic in a BEGINNER's course. Just buy a DVD from any store.

Great professor, very caring about his students learning. There'll always be 1 or 2 unhappy students whose comments are a function of their own poor performance or personality issues. Many students appreciate the structure and organization the textbook provides. There are plenty of activities not involving the textbook -- a good balance overall.

analysis.name: Alexander Byrd
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dropped class.  Exceedingly biased. Felt if didn't agree with his views would be difficult to get a good grade. Too much work for subject &  level and many assignments  convoluted in directions. Takes what could be a interesting fun class and turns into boring grind. AVOID

Prof. Byrd was one of the best professors I had at Rice. He was my honors history thesis advisor and he was very helpful and informed. My experience at Rice would have been completely different had I not had him as a teacher. He will challenge you intellectually and support your academic growth. TAKE HIS CLASSES.
Really good lectures and interesting class discussions.
definitely take any class that he teaches! he is so understanding and helpful, and really challenges you to think more analytically, asks really thought-provoking questions.
Helps students become better writers.  Very accessible.

analysis.name: Beatriz Gonzalez-Stephan
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

analysis.name: Logan Browning
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: So kind and flexible as a professor, discussions were always so interesting in his class and I learned a lot, even beyond the scope of the syllabus material, as Prof Browning was so knowledgeable about all the course material. 100% recommend!

He can spend the entire class wondering off tangents on really boring things. For example if a character in the textbook has a bulldog he'll start talking about his dog for the rest of the class. He never seems to understand students when they say their opinions, instead he says something totally unrelated and says that's what they just said.

Dr. Browning creates a relaxed intellectual atmosphere that focuses on discussion instead of direct lecture. You learn through yourself and other students - it's nice class to balance with other lecture based courses.

analysis.name: James Brown
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Quite possibly the best economics professor at Rice. Classes were engaging, and I never missed a single one. Brown makes the undeniably boring subject of Microeconomics fun. EXTREMELY tough on tests. Aims for a 60% average. Weekly problem sets are given, but not required, as he gives you the answers. Highly recommed taking his classes.

Definitely my favorite professor at Rice; he's accessible by cell phone, brilliant at what he does, and hilarious. He cares about his students and learns all 100 or so names. He offered support and amazing words of encouragement to me when I was having mental health issues during the semester. Take the class, and get ready for a lot of calculus
Best econ professor ever. Enthusiastic and passionate about the subject. Extremely helpful. No take-home assignment. Unconventional midterms. Do not miss class or you will not be able to pass exams.

analysis.name: Brian Huberman
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Be prepared to watch a lot of western films that are sometimes questionable. I enjoyed most of them but I grew up watching Bonanza. The only grades are three essays. If you're not a senior your final paper is due almost two weeks after the last day of class which was very nice. Overall, an easy Distribution 1 credit.

Amazing professor!  Gave me the foundation for my professional and academic career.  In the years after graduating from Rice, I've met dozens of former student's who say Huberman's courses were the highlight of their undergrad education!
Best professor I've had.  Inspired and supported my career.

analysis.name: Cronin Justin
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: a lot of technical help with stories, not the vague emotional stuff that you get in many creative-writing courses.  unlike most Rice classes, this one is a good place to make friends.  it's always fun, and it creates a community of serious (or semi-serious) writers.
Awesome teacher... brilliant, and fun to have in class.

analysis.name: Jon Chaika
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I enjoyed my Linear Algebra taught by Professor Chaika. He is proof-heavy and uses the chalkboard more than any professor I've ever seen, but I feel like he genuinely wants his students to succeed and will hold midterm reviews that last as long as you need them to. Just do your reading and you'll be fine.

If anyone needs to take linear algebra from the U PLEASE do yourself a favor and take it from Jon. He is one of the best professors and people that I have ever met. He is the sweetest and most genuine man in the world. I STRONGLY Encourage anyone to try a class with Jon. All the negative comments are from students who are unwilling to accept grades

Jon is HANDS down one of the best professors I have ever had. I could not understand at all before I took his class. Now, I am a math major. I love him so much and I am so thankful for the way he teaches. We need more teachers like Jon. TAKE HIS CLASS!!!!!

I took Linear Algebra 2270 from Prof Chaika at the U of Utah (He doesn't teach at Rice anymore). Great guy. Really good at doing math, horrible at teaching it. This guy is too smart to be teaching undergrad classes. He is not capable of dumbing down concepts well enough to understand. Such a nice, caring man, but not a good undergrad professor.

Kindly, this is the least workable math instructing I've had. I had him for calculus 1210, but that wasn't an option in the dropdown. Anything I learned, it's because I taught myself outside of class. I've never met someone who can make such a simple concept seem impossible in the way he can (why I learned better when I taught myself). Don't take.

analysis.name: Vicki Colvin
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

analysis.name: David Caprette
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: While the labs are very well designed, caprette seems to have no interest in actually helping his students.  While I have no problem with harsh grading, he seems to give arbitrary evaluations, with contradictory comments across assignments, and ignores requests for help (not regrades!) with curt, rude denials rather than constructive criticism.

Very clear and a fair grader - really wants  students to understand the methodology and be able to apply it in other situations.  Good preparation for more advanced lab courses.  Challenging, but worth it.
it's easy to get an A if you just do what he asks. he's very specific, so if you follow his directions to the letter you'll be fine. although the work is time consuming.
This class will eat your time.  I found Caprette to be very nice as long as you didn't do anything stupid, but that website of his is hell to search through.

He is perhaps the worst professor you will ever have.  And he denies extra help for assignments, leaving you on your own except for his website which is like a maze.  Although I don't mind that his comments aren't too flattering, he can be rather rude at times for very arbitrary mistakes that could easily happen during the lab.

analysis.name: Craig Considine
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: 10/10 would recommend. You should take at least one class with Considine in your undergraduate career as he gives you a lot of autonomy in how you utilize the classroom space and gives you room to grow as a person and as an individual.

Craig goes above and beyond what any other professor I've had ever has. If you ask a question in class he'll follow up with an email after class offering even more information and plenty of links to follow. He is incredibly invested in teaching and in his students' learning experiences. If anything he almost devotes too much of his own time to help

Professor Considine is probably one of the greatest instructors I've had at Rice. Not only is he extremely knowledgeable about the subject matter, you can tell he's truly passionate about what he teaches. What sets him apart is his commitment to his students. Soon after the first day of class, he emailed me personally to elaborate on a topic I brie

Everyone needs to take a class with Dr. Considine. He is very passionate about what he teaches and does a great job of engaging with students and fostering discussion in class. He is also so so nice and encouraging to his students. He is really supportive of everyone and ensures that everyone has what they need in order to succeed in the class.

Dr. Considine is an incredible professor and a valuable mentor. He is by far one of my favorite professors at Rice, and I'm glad I was able to have him guide us on this independent study over the course of the semester. It was an incredibly rewarding experience have Dr. Considine guide us through an independent research project.

analysis.name: Eric Chesebro
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of those rare people that clearly "gets" difficult math concepts without needing them to be explained, so doesn't want to explain it to students. Doesn't believe in showing you how to do something, but will only provide hints for you to try to work things out - it's not even worth going to him for help. TONS of homework & tests are super hard.
Great teacher, usually comes up with lots of examples and explanations to help out. The tests are not that bad.
He wasn't too bad, but sometimes he had trouble getting down on our level to explain it since he's so smart.

analysis.name: Luis Duno-Gottberg
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: My fav professor at Rice! His dedication is incredible, and his teaching style is both fun and original. He cares about the process or how we reason through problems more than just getting “the right final answer”. He'll often switch sides in an argument to test different ideas and push the class to really defend our reasoning.  I honestly love it.

This is easily one of the best professors I've had throughout my undergraduate education. His classes really encourage you to think outside the box and challenge the way you approach the subject. If you're open to new ideas and willing to engage critically with the material, I would highly recommend taking his classes.

f you're ready to be challenged and like thinking beyond the usual, Luis is the instructor you want. He's super knowledgeable but also kind and humble. His class pushes you past superficial views of Latin American history and politics by questioning assumptions.

Challenging, provocative, and inspiring. His classes constantly push you to consider ideas you never thought possible. You leave his class not with easy answers, but with better questions. LL (Love Luis) Gime me an A next time though.
From an academic standpoint, his credentials are outstanding. That said, his active support of the Cuban and Venezuelan regimes warrants careful consideration.

analysis.name: Stanley Dodds
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He does not lecture at all. Instead, he provides us with a bunch of long, wordy documents, and it can take hours to complete a single assignment. He is never available for office hours, and it's difficult to find him except just minutes before a deadline.
Should he do the experiment, he will know how time-consuming it is with only one credit

Not nearly as mean as he seems. He's just intimidating. He's one of the few physics professors knowledgeable about undergraduate coursework. Talk to him if you need advice on courses. The labs can be difficult, just be sure to read the lab documents all the way through.
Take this class because Dr. Hannon is good and the lecture is enjoyable.  The lab is a necessary evil and Dr. Dodds SUCKS!!  Alas, there are only seven labs and you will finish with him eventually...

nearly always be in his office when we needed him -which was often- even at somewhat odd hours. very, very, VERY patient and helpful, and remembered my name though i still am not sure how or when he learned it.

analysis.name: Don Johnson
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Genius! Pure genius, this man.
DoJo is a pillar of the ECE community at Rice.
DoJo is the greatest ELEC professor on planet Earth.

analysis.name: Scott Derrick
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Professor Derrick is a wonderful person and incredible teacher. His lectures are always entertaining and unorthodox because of the different perspectives he brings. Veering off-topic during lecture is part of the beauty of his teaching style. His feedback is thorough and insightful, and very fair with multiple opportunities for error correction.

He is excellent! I actually had him for English @ New Trier H.S. years ago.  So different from the humorless drones I was used to. He made me love literature that I might otherwise have found boring.   My fave teacher ever! I've often wondered where he wound up.  He's offbeat but brilliant! If you get a chance to take him don't hesitate!

Never prepared lessons or presented necessary historical context. We wallowed in the shallow end of the pool all semester, fielding the most obvious jargon from the loudest among us. How can one teach a four-month class on 19th c. US without Melville? Hmmm... (long, empty pause, staring out window.)

Don't take this class unless you want to be creeped out.  He brought in materials featuring Dr. Spock (Star Trek) nude and in the mood, and we spent an hour and a half discussing it.  I can't remember why this was listed as an English class.

I love Derrick! Definitely take 300 with him. Most other professors make you learn the theorists by wrote memorization. He can be a little spacey, but he keeps things engaging and interesting. Great prof.

analysis.name: Devika Subramanian
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: My experience, about 4 years ago, was that her classes were poorly organized. She jumped from one complex topic to another, without a lot of intuition to help students along. She's very energetic, and has a lot of passion for her subject. But, assignments didn't come back graded in time and there wasn't much appreciation for the subject conveyed.

Devika is one of the friendliest teachers I have come across at Rice. She is quite good at explaining difficult concepts, and is very patient when speaking to students outside of class. Don't even hesitate to take a class with her.
I learned a lot from Prof. Subramanian. She is a great teacher and professor.

Dr. Subramanian is alert, and catches subtle statements easily, but in the same way, expects you to listen to what she says. Sometimes she gives a hint by what she does NOT say. She's fair and attentive to student, but does not like slackers.

analysis.name: Eugene Zubarev
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I learned a lot from Dr Zubarev's class. His lectures are amazing and perfectly clear. One of the best professors I had at Rice.

Zubarev knows what hes lecturing about and can answer any question, but be cautious. He gets angry easily and often makes rude remarks. Additionally, if hes in a bad mood hell just take points off tests or regrades for the heck of it. Worst, his lectures are insanely dry. As someone who got an A, I wish he had stuck to research & not teaching.

Take this class if you want any A and still learn a lot. If you premed, the course by Zubarev is perfect. He taught it for so many years and knows exactly how to delivery this smilingly tough subjects. The exams were reasonable, but his grading is very generous. Review sessions were extremely helpful. Highly recommend this prof.
Best Orgo professor at Rice, no question about it. Take 212 with him if you can.

Dr. Zubarev knows a lot about what hes talking about but he isnt very good at explaining it in lecture. Hes very temperamental and his lectures are very disorganized. Do not take this class with him unless you have no other option. Trust me its not worth it.

analysis.name: Erzsebet Merenyi
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Get ready to do things HER way. As most Rice professors are, Dr. Merenyi is helpful in accommodating dates for her students; nevertheless, it will be under her conditions and these must be met if you intend on getting a full grade. Dr. Merenyi also likes to use her own terms instead of the ones commonly used in machine learning.

Prof Merenyi is a consummate professional and a wonderful professor.  I have taken several classes from her.  If you're looking for an easy grade, its time to grow up and put on your grown up pants... welcome to grad school! Stick with her courses, you'll learn a lot!  I am now faculty at a major university and model my courses after hers!

analysis.name: Sarah Ellenzweig
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: "Absolutely worthless"? Wow, that's just blatantly untrue. I thought Dr. Ellenzweig was an excellent prof. We had engaging discussions, she was always enthusiastic and helpful, and most of the readings were fun. Yes, the class was easy, and I didn't feel like my writing changed that much through it, but that's just the nature of a mixed intro class

I thought this class was very interesting and our discussions were extremely helpful. I would recommend taking this class if you are genuinely interested and are planning on going to class because it's hard to catch up/understand if you don't. She is a very interesting lecturer and reallly knows her stuff. I definitely recommend her!

Dr. Ellenzweig is an amazing professor.  Her lectures are engaging and she has an interesting reading list.  She also very helpful when you are writing essays.  She makes helpful suggestions and my writing skills improved tremendously in her class.

A nice lady, readings were often interesting, grades essays fairly generously/not especially insightfully, yes discussions were mindnumbingly dull, I don't know whether that was the fault of the prof or the mix of people.
Very engaging, interesting personality, cares about students

analysis.name: Elena Pavelescu
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. But trending upward recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I had this professor for MATH 102 at Rice University and I can attest she was an extraordinary teacher! She always had a smile on her face and always taught the concepts with utmost clarity.  Her face brightened up the world and her passion inspired me to pursue a career as an artist.  She taught me the value of passion.
She is the best professor I've had! She teaches everything in a simple way that is easy to understand and will always help you during office hours. Her sense of humor made the class fun too.
Extremely clear and helpful. Obviously loves her subject and cares about the students.
She's helpful and very nice, you can tell she's pulling for you to do well.

analysis.name: Frank Jones
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment:
One of the best professors at Rice. He makes me love mathematics and want to pursue it even more. He is funny, caring, inspirational; you would definitely love this lovely man.
Be aware that his lectures focus on mathematical thinking rather than solving problems, so extra practice is needed.

Legend at Rice. I am so grateful to be in his last course here!
Truly inspirational, caring, and enthusiastic about Mathematics.
"Future Rice students will never know Frank Jones anymore..."
"So be it!" says Frank.

Dr. Jones is possibly the greatest teacher I've ever had. His preparations for class are outstanding, his lectures are entertaining and he makes any mathematical topic interesting. It's clear that he loves math, teaching, and his students. I've had 4 classes with him and he's done an excellent job with all. If you have any chance, take his class.

The first class I ever had at Rice (Fall '89) was Frank Jones' Math 212, which met MWF at 7:45. It is a sign of his lecture style that I missed maybe one lecture the whole semester. He was obviously excited about teaching and an excellent lecturer.   Take any Frank Jones course you can while he's still there!

I took several classes (Diff. Eqs., Multivariable Calc, Real Analysis) from Dr Jones over 25 years ago (1975-78), but he was great then and it sounds like he is still great now!  He probably made it possible for me to succeed as a math major when I otherwise might not have been able to complete that degree.

analysis.name: Farinaz Koushanfar
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

analysis.name: Rudy Guerra
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Makes everything super simple. When I didn't go to class, I could easily catch up with his pre-recorded videos on canvas and just with that I was able to get an A+. You're allowed to bring a cheat sheet into the final exam, which makes everything easy as long as you know when to use what. Exams are generally similar to problem sets as well.

Absolute GOAT.

If you ever have the chance to take Professor Rudy Guerra, DO IT. I have never met a math/statistics professor who is simultaneously this brilliant and this invested in making sure students actually understand the material.

There is little to no organization to his course. His pacing when he lectures is absolutely awful, and is never able to cover topics in depth - but makes the problem sets and exams much more difficult. Also, his handwriting is really bad
I am niece so be nice to my uncle you all. He is just my funny uncle. His daughters and one son is super smart. I don't care you don't like him but he' s family so give him respect!

Dr. Guerra's classes are confusing, and his handwriting is not legible.  He was a nice guy and encouraged students to come see him.  The tests in the class were like a race, and I was never able to finish a good portion of them.  The class is really difficult if you're not a stat genius, but he does curve a lot.

analysis.name: Michelle Gilbertson
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I took General Chemistry II with her and found the course to be awfully organized, deeply confusing, and frustrating to navigate. Lectures were haphazard and not representative of test material. The time devoted to equally important topics was inconsistent. This was detrimental to my learning process.

Hated this lecture. Prof used a tablet to lecture. Poor handwriting and lots of mistakes. Very eager to help during her limited office hours. Even pre-exam review sessions were held by TAs (also lackluster).  Timed, weekly quizzes for homework which I hated but really helped prep for exams. Be ready to spend lots of time in front of your orgo text.
She is GREAT. Understands the material and gives very clear lectures. She really seems to care about us leaning the material.

analysis.name: Zhiyong Gao
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 28 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: His eccentricity is well-known, but Gao's an effective professor with some legendary quips. On a more serious note, he makes great use of lecture time to explain each topic thoroughly and he answers questions well. Your experience with him will be great if you show up to class and take your notes.

Funniest teacher you will ever have. The day before exams, he goes over everything that will be on the exam. And not just what might be on the exam. He literally numbers the topics as he reviews them, and the numbers match up with questions on the exam.
Great at entertaining he class, not so great at teaching it...doesnt do much explaining...and the homework sets can get tiresome...first two tests are a breee though
Professor Gao is amazing. Highly recommend. He makes even a difficult and tedious class, Multivariable Calculus, seem fun and easy. Lectures are hysterical too.

Professor Gao has been the most caring professor I have ever have in college. He really makes himself available to help students outside of class. His office hours always end up being a collaboration space where other students come together and he guides our learning. He is very understanding and will guide classes with great questions.

analysis.name: Mike Gustin
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Gustin was awesome. Even years later, I still remember his lectures and how engaging they were. I am amazed how interesting he made cell bio and his old-school methods of writing and drawing on the chalk-board instead of using powerpoints. Super cool guy and really respect him as a professor.

Dr. Gustin is honestly so nice, and I would absolutely recommend him to anyone's who's interested in Biology. We're a class of about 150 students but he still takes the time to try and get to know each and every one of us. The grading for his classes is also ACTUALLY designed to help students pass as long as the work hard and stay on top of things.
Very good lecturer, but the class is very deep. Hard when you don't have an AP Bio background.

Really nice guy. Lectures were decent, keeping in mind he is covering SO much elementary Biology material. Tests were a tad bit confusing and method of grading them seemed rather unfair. Make sure to go to his review sessions the night before the exam.

Gustin really cares about teaching and it shows.  He may not be the "coolest" guy around, but he's very approachable and helpful.  Lectures were never a waste of time, which is hard to do in an intro bio class.   Tests challenging, but fair.

analysis.name: Richard Grandy
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Not a class to skip--do so once and you will fall behind and never recover. Overall I enjoyed the class a lot. Also: GREAT D3, for those of you who don't want to put up with hard science (which are all exceptionally lame, despite what everyone else at Rice says).
I graduated from Rice four years ago, and Professor Grandy is still the most inspiring professor I've ever met.
Cleverest professor ever seen in Rice Philosophy Department. Unfortunately he is retiring this year.

analysis.name: Kyle Goyette
analysis.verdict: Great professor — tough but students love the teaching. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: A highly intellectual man that is able to help you understand policy on another level with sophisticated arguments in reference to historical individuals and events.   Wish to have another opportunity to debate with professor Goyette again.
I hate history and this is been my favorite class so far. His lectures are hilarious. Exams or essay and pretty tough but he lays out the expectations so you know what to expect. Overall great class.
Cold War research seminar was one of the best classes I've ever taken.  Dr. G is an incredible prof.  Didn't think a class on the Cold War could be so much fun to attend but I never missed a class.
Loved this class and professor.  Lectures are fun.  Essay exams and a term paper but he gives you a review sheet before the exams to help you study.  Would take any class he teaches.
Cold War class was awesome.  He's really passionate about the material and makes it super interesting.  Loved coming to class each time.

analysis.name: HENGRUI LUO
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He is an expert in Bayesian optimization, but it is really amazing that he shed light on the design perspective I learned from a previous course; also a wonderful project experience working on the project with the class folks, best teacher ever!
This is one of the best high-dimensional statistics course I have ever taken, making the whole Wainwright's book like an interesting sci-fi...
Very clean and good at creating intuitions, very detailed memory (the guy remember what is written on the whiteboard couple of lectures ago), easy to work with.

Doing some non-course research project with henry. He's very detailed with nice clarification and notes (which helps!), his voice is kind of soft but he is a really kind person. Getting (truly) interested in research in statistics now lol
He is very good at explaining math, a nice guy BUT be aware of the dense HWs and random question he throws at you!

analysis.name: Jason Hafner
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Extremely funny! Lectures are great, easy to understand, and you can definitely keep up with him. His humor only adds to the enjoyment of learning the subject.   The course was organized very well. Good balance of theory and problem solving, and Jason was active throughout and helping answer doubts, making this one of the best courses I took.

Professor Hafner is an amazing lecturer. He's honestly hilarious, which makes his lectures really engaging. I thought the course was conceptually tough, but his explanations helped a lot. Plus, the textbook was great. My main complaint is that the in-class exams required the double time available for them, so they felt rushed.

Really poor professor. He does not help students at all. The homework and exams were jokes, as they do not reflect what we learned in class. DO NOT TAKE HIS CLASS. He is funny for sure, but his course material is so useless.

He is not teaching. Neither does it give any useful derivation or problem-solving techniques. His handwriting is not recognizable, and the textbook is a joke. Do not take his class if you can. He was a great and enthusiastic professor but not ANYMORE.
The professor does not give any useful information. He does not hold enough office hours, and no one can understand his published lecture notes.

analysis.name: Kim  Hooge
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She gives grade to project that she wants even without looking at the criteria. She never checks her email, and you can't find her after class even in the office hour.

This review is for PSYC 340 - Dr. OH is just about the more student-oriented professor there is: flexible extensions, caring, non-mandatory attendance, and overall just super chill. Would definitely take this course again if I could. Hoping to take Memory with Dr. OH in a future semester.

She has great demos during lecture that makes the topic memorable and fun. The group project is similar to a final, you get to choose your group and present a demo of your choice. (Online or in person)

analysis.name: Mikki Hebl
analysis.verdict: Solid pick — well-liked with reasonable difficulty. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Took her class years back when I was doing a BA Psych at Rice. She's the definition of a performative white feminist. She seemed out of touch with real-time issues and didn't understand intersectionality at times. Every revelation she had was painfully surface-level and her activism was superficial.
didn't like the teaching format

Loved her lectures, tests were fair, pretty fun class. Journals were useless busy work and got annoying very quickly. Had no problem til the end of the semester when she miscalculated my grade. Very hard to get a hold of her to this to fix this, multiple emails before any response. Probably won't take her class again because of the grading error.

Made class fun.  My only major critism is the lack of the accountability she puts on the TAs. Check over your grade calculation at the end of the class--my grade was .5 too low due to a silly calculation error and it was corrected to bring my grade up.  A few other students had this problem.

Amazing teacher, genuinely one of the nicest people I have ever met. She has a sort of enthusiastic aura that perks you up even when you're tired. She tries to make very fair tests and does fun class demonstrations. Definitely recommend taking a class with her.

analysis.name: Margeret Hennessy

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Hennessy is a really nice professor who is willing to help students with bonus points for participation. She's always willing to answer questions for students. Visiting her during office hours and going to review sessions before the test will help a lot. She is tough but fair.
She might not be the best lecturer, but she's really helpful if you go in for office hours. a down to earth and really sweet person. oh - and apparently a lot of guys like her ^^ haha
Shes an excellent prof. her office hrs r very flexible so shes usually available.  She wants everyone to do good in the class and takes interest in all the students.
nice person, terrible prof. she's a little more helpful explaining concepts if you go see her during office hours one on one.
material gworl. good @ teaching! likes coffee and her child (hopefully). coldplay #stan.

KAHOOT! hes very theatrical, engaging & isnt afraid to get awkward to get an answer. Overall, I would recommend & wish Ill have him again. THREE CHEERS FOR THOMAS! *hilarious *

If you are up for the intellecutal rigor this professor demands, and are interested in subjects like the basis of democracy and why it can't be exported like aluminum, the mechanics and basis of Amnerican Capitalism, and the foundations of American society, you should find him, as I have, one of the best professors Rice has to offer.

I don't know, maybe I went into this class expecting the wrong things...I mean the guys IS teaching about puritans which is pretty much a snoozefest in itself.  All I can say with all certainty and honesty is that I couldn't go to this class without falling asleep.  Coffee intake and a full night's sleep couldn't stop me.

Amazing class! This guy is awesome. Very engaging. Knows how to handle a class discussion. He's also more than willing to meet with you outside of class. Top notch.

hutch is AWSOME!!!!!!!!!!!! take him!

If you wanna go to bible college and learn chemistry, James.... James is the lad.

I have too many stories from I tell when picking up ladies cause their so funny!!

analysis.name: J Tour
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time — consider alternatives if possible

One test question asked about the 2010 nobel prize. Also, the quizzes took time away from practicing the problems in the book; the problems in the book provided more benefit than the quizzes.
Terrible professor. Constantly injects religion and politics into his lectures. Can't believe students have to be subjected to this and with such a hard subject.
Not a good prof...teaches orgo straight from the book and if you don't understand it there, you're screwed.

More of a preacher than a teacher, professor Tour can't help but his religion into his lectures. Honestly not sure this guy should even be teaching ochem, since he doesn't even believe in abiogenesis. Embarrassment to the university.
He was great but he seemed distant and I relied solely on the other teachers for help

review_highlights.comment: Absolutely THE worst professor I have ever had through out my collage career, he answers students' question on piazza very randomly and most of the answers are just copy-pastes from the rubric, and when it comes to more specific question he always throw out the "your decision" answer. Lectures are just reading his ppt from the past years.

Worst course and professor I have in my college career. Project spec is too general. Takes nearly a day to respond students' questions on Piazza. Don't listen to any students' feedback. If you are not familiar with front end dev, ready for being overwhelmed, because he provides zero help with it. DON'T TAKE HIS COURSE UNLESS YOU HAVE TO.

The worst professor at Rice Univ I have ever seen. Don't take his class unless you have to! Very heavy workload on assignments which you don't learn much from it except how to finish a 2 months project in one week.

Dr. Kemmer is very passionate about linguistics and the history of the English language. Her tests are very random and consist of many details in her lectures. I recommend taking great lecture notes or even recording lectures to catch everything that she goes over in class.
Boring lectures. Random details on exams. Not a very interesting class at all. Not a bad class to Pass/Fail.

I loved Dr. Kabiri! I took her online anatomy class and learned a lot. It was a bit difficult to retain information between its online structure and rapid pace, but Dr. Kabiri's jokes and enthusiasm made the class worthwhile. Classes are divided between fill-in-the-blank notes and VisionLab sections. Quizlet is your best friend!
Take her class!! She offers so many options to retake anything you get a bad grade on and also drops your lowest grades. She is so sweet and engaging.

analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge

review_highlights.comment: He is easily the best teacher I've ever had. He presents interesting but easy to understand examples. He intentionally appears as very mean at times but it is just an illusion to keep the class interesting and everyone on their toes. Never had a dull moment in the class.
Luay is amazing! Best cs Prof at Rice

I took 182 a while back, the class material wasn't hard (easier than "hard" leetcode at tier 1 tech interviews), but the graders were unnecessarily harsh and it really made me question whether CS was right for me. 

I am glad I hold onto CS, now I'm doing very well in my engineering career.

A lot of people call him funny, but when he makes the same joke over and over again about students failing the class, it gets pretty annoying. He's pretty good at teaching I guess, but the material is hard, so you still need a lot of studying. I personally thought he made the class too difficult and didn't leave us any time to have that study time.

Good at simplifying things and giving concrete examples Asks questions similar to hw questions in the exam Hints questions (not covered in lectures) one week before the midterm and asks in the midterm exam Doesn't really have office hours, too busy Doesn't know how to pick good TAs Can cancel 6 classes in a semester Cares about attendance too much

Instead of techniques for calculation, his lectures focus on how matrix is connected with linear transformation.
The professor does not use any textbook.
Siran Li is pretty hard to understand. Not because of his accent, but because of his lecturing style.

review_highlights.comment: One of the best Professor I have had at Rice. He makes Mechanics fun to learn. He is very knowledgeable and presents the concepts in very clear and concise manner. He presents practical examples of mechanics.
Dr. Satish is a great teacher. He's professional, organized, and presents complex material in a way that simplifies. He's also an expert in this material. Learn from Satish.
Dr. Nagarajaiah single handedly made this the most useful and helpful class I've taken in the mechanical engineering sequence.
Dr. Nagarajaiah is one of the best Professors I have had at Rice. I really enjoyed taking Mechanics of Solids course.
Outstanding Professor. Worth taking this course. One of the best.

review_highlights.comment: DNC is great. She really cares about you and really takes it on herself to make sure you understand everything. Amazing person
DNC was a great prof...very approachable and always willing to help with whatever you needed.
Super nice, really helpful and really cares about the students.
DNC is the bomb! If you are looking for a professor that is kind and helpful regardless of your french-speaking background, look no further!

analysis.name: Linda Neagley
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Neagley is friendly, approachable, smart, and passionate about her field. She is very flexible and adapts class sessions to our interest. Grading was very fair and she gives good feedback. Some lectures were a little dry but sometimes you just have to get through the material. I would recommend her for anyone interested in architecture.

While she is obviously extremely knowledgeable, she tends to meander wildly around various topics, rarely pausing for long enough to allow comprehensive note-taking or understanding.  She babbles inanely at length about minutiae and then must rush to catch up, skimping on important topics.  She's eager to help though (albeit ineptly).

She does go off on long tangents in her lectures and then is forced to skip out on many important topics which she then will test you on, though she is willing to help, but i say beware the neaglemeister
I have never seen so many people asleep around one table before.  This woman is the human form of Nytol.  Her classes always sound interesting.  But stay away!  She's incredibly boring.
lol. Check the rating below. It's EXACTLY right! Add to that the fact that your friends in that class will probably be sleeping not far from you.

analysis.name: John Olson
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews — take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Olson is an awesome professor. He works hard to make a difficult subject accessible.  He shows up early to classes to help students, he often stays late, and he makes himself very available to all his students outside of class.  He's probably one of the most straight-forward professors you'll ever have, and he grades & tests very fairly.

Dr. Olson is an awesome guy. He knows that the material he teaches is hard and accommodates students so that they can learn the material and not die at the same time. He's a really nice guy and really cares about his students!
Dr. Olson is extremely nice, he will show up to class like an hour before the actual time just to answer questions...most importantly he is a dream boat...his wife is a lucky lady...

analysis.name: Philip Wood
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews — check if this fits your learning style

review_highlights.comment: JACKASS. Rude. Demeaning. Assigns altogether too much work. Makes fun of the slower students in class. Tells you how much of a drag it is to read your papers. Plays favorites. Expects you to spend more time on his class than any other class you may be in. He knows his stuff, though - definitely smart.

Avoid taking Professor Wood's class if you value your self-respect. He had a profoundly negative impact on my entire experience as a student at Rice. Professor Wood is a bitter individual who appears to be unhappy with his own life and directs his anger towards his students.
Hopefully things have changed since I was in his class. He is definitely rude and unhelpful! I'd take pretty much any prof over him.
This was a very weird class indeed. His ideas were interesting at first, but then they got boring and repetitive.

Prof. Wood challenges students to search within, not only to investigate the questions presented by the material in class, but to arrive at a higher level of awareness. The process is uncomfortable and very difficult, and that is exactly how you arrive at gaining anything of value for your own personal growth.

review_highlights.comment: Great spanish teacher. She cares for her students and won't make you look like an idiot when doing class activities. It's very important that you attend class and do the homework as the pace is quick. Would recommend, but only if you're willing to work.
Great teacher, very nice to students. Not afraid is use "adult" words or talk about "adult" themes--she knows we're in college.
Nice teacher, lots of busywork, but not her fault. Span 101 syllabus not created by her or flexible.
I learned a lot; she was nice and understanding. She was normal for a Spanish teacher.
GREAT PROF!  She really cares for her students.

Speaks very fast and can be incomprehensible. Keeps most of the course work for the end of the semester. Not organized.
loses the class a lot. snooze fest most of the time.

Dr. Radigan is definitely one of the best professors at Rice. Everyone should take her class.

Probably my favorite professor ever!

analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students. And trending downward

review_highlights.comment: Prof Rixner is an excellent engaging lecturer.  He communicates clearly and concisely.  He possesses a sense of humor and playfulness  with the material that maintains my interest throughout the lecture.  The guy knows what he's doing.  I've been teaching for over 20 years.  He's a keeper.

I took his coursera course to learn Python. Wow, he is good. Not only that he knows the stuff, but he also knows how to teach - he is super clear, funny, and knows how to engage students with his assignments and projects. I have taken so many online classes, and he is definitely one of the best professors!
Great professor! He keeps his lectures clear and interesting, especially with his ties and his owl!

great professor!! very easy to follow. he's very funny as well!

analysis.name: Robert Werth
analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge

review_highlights.comment: Werth is one of those incredibly intelligent professors who also happens to know how to impart his knowledge on his students. He is very helpful outside of class - responds quickly to emails, always willing to squeeze you into his very popular office hours, genuinely interested in what you want to knowask.

Great class, and great professor. One of my favorites. Period. There was a lot of reading and work, so don't fall behind. But class was great. Prof knows his stuff, and is nice also. Approachable. He encourages participation, almost socratic style. One of my favorite college classes.
Werth is a great professor and really wants you to succeed. He is tough and reading and participation are mandatory but he is understanding and engaging in class.
He explains the difficult concepts very well. He is kind and understanding, and wants his students to succeed. Take his class if you can!

analysis.name: Stephen Zeff
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews — experiences vary a lot between students

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Zeff is a mean, malicious and egocentric person. A normal professor wouldnt go to another professors office and say bad things about his students. A normal professor would also not use malicious words in the email to his students, saying things like his class is more important than your job interviews. He does not deserve to be a professor.

Always in office hours. Sends tons of email updates (not sure why). Obviously very knowledgeable. Could probably go a little bit easier on people when they don't now the answer, but I think it's just that he's old and that's probably how they used to teach.
Very mean to students. Not sure why. A great teacher though, but his teaching method may not be the best.

Zeff is actually a pretty good professor. His lectures are excellent. He explains things very well and has clear expectations for tests. That being said, he is very abrasive and is quite harsh if you don't answer his questions correctly when he calls on you. Just make sure you're prepared with correct answers; have friends who can help you out.
Sends emails that are uncalled for and uses disrespectful language to address students.

simply does not know how to support a class. his lectures flip flop and his slides have no discernable content on them. if he doesn't like you, he won't answer any of your questions and he tends to be quite rude. he's a middle eastern history professor who pronounces "iranian" incorrectly. so mean for no reason.

Professor Makdisi is truly an inspirational professor. I've told so many people that his lectures are like TED talks because he is so passionate in his field. He's an amazing communicator, and he has informed my perspective of the world in a way that no other class has in the past.

I took Makdisi's class while he was a visiting professor at UC Berkeley. His lectures are very captivating; everyone is listening at all times. He's a phenomenal lecturer and speaker, although I cannot say much about his grading habits, as my TA was responsible for grading exams.
Extremely knowledgeable about his field, truly an amazing person to learn from. He is tough in terms of grading, but he has amazing readings and engaging class discussions

brilliant prof, great teacher. v. interesting, able to keep class hooked, teaches subject well and explains problems v. well. highly recommended for any class!!

review_highlights.comment: In terms of research, he is very cutthroat. He will do anything for grant money to beat out his competitors, even if it means playing unfairly. It's not personal; it's just business. Great professor in Biochem though.

Very nice guy, pretty funny as well.  Great to talk to about grad schools and the like if that's what you need.  Yes he makes you memorize the amino acids, but get over it.  The class is interesting, and I'd still say it's more conceptually based than memorization based.  Especially compared to evil bios 302.  Tests are challenging, but it was nice to actually be challenged on a test and get a chance to show what I learned.

Great professor who forces certain things to be memorized, all of which are actually useful. He also tests over the material in a clear and straightforward manner.  His review sessions are not a waste of time-he actually cares that you learn the material.

Good guy.  I got screwed on the curve, and he wouldn't help me out, but I still think he is a good guy and a good teacher.  I'm sure if you went to him for help, he'd be helpful, I just wouldn't know for sure.
He's a nice guy and pretty good at lecturing. Tests are too hard for the material he presents. Visit him in the office, don't take his class.

review_highlights.comment: The material was very nicely motivated and explained. Massoud is just an amazing professor and really knows how to get you hooked. It was really enjoyable to learn how to design a hearing aid as the class project. I highly recommend the class to engineering students.

This is the best professor I have encountered in Rice. He will challenge you and he will demand excellence. He does that while maintaining a fun atmosphere. I highly recommend his classes. You will learn a lot while having a big smile!

The only thing he does not look like is a teacher. His English is poor and unclear. Most of the sessions are taught by his students. no notes, no organization. He tells very lame jokes! Wanna waste you time, take a course with him.
Great prof! High energy, knows his stuff, loves it and will make it love it too :)  Enjoyed the class project immensely. Highly recommend!

analysis.verdict: Good teacher but expect a challenge. And trending downward
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews — decent sample but could shift

review_highlights.comment: Awesome professor!  Great lectures and very detailed, but if you are the type to be absent a lot, or skip, you'll lose a lot of notes and have a hard time keeping up.   He was always willing to help and explain, and fairly easy to get in touch with.  HIGHLY RECOMMEND!!!   Can't say enough good things about him.

Dr. Zammito is an amazing and articulate professor.  He is a brilliant scholar and engaging lecturer whose passion for Intellectual History is contagious.  His "European Intellectual History" class has truly been a watershed moment in my undergraduate career - it has revolutionized my worldview.

Best lecturer I have ever seen.  There was actually applause at the end of the first class.  The material is pretty tough but if you put in the effort you can do well.  This is one class that I would actually go to even if I wasn't getting graded on the materal--he makes everything that interesting.

Had him for history of World War 1 at Texas in 1985.  Spellbinding lecturer.  Best history teacher I ever had, and I took a LOT of history classes.  He really knows and understands European history, especially intellectual history and art for the period immediately before and  after World War 1.

He knows his facts, but his lectures are quite opinionated and soapbox-styled.  He usually makes arguments about abstract concepts that somehow relate to his disdain for Christianity and imperialism.  He's also very passionate about expressing his opinions in class- very unprofessional.
